steps.title: Identify the point being compared
steps.content: The charity with the greatest percent of expenses spent on programs is the highest point on the scatterplot, about $(3400,92)$. The actual percent is therefore about $92$.

steps.title: Estimate the fit line at that x-value
steps.content: Use the drawn line of best fit to predict the percent at $x\approx3400$. A Desmos check using approximate points on the fit line gives $y\approx0.00184x+78.8$.

steps.title: Compute the residual
steps.content: At $x=3400$, the model predicts about $0.00184(3400)+78.8\approx85$. The actual point is about $92$, so the difference is $92-85=7$ percentage points. Choice B is correct.
